title: Duplicate charges for enterprise tenant
raw_text: [14:02] @sre-oncall we are seeing multiple Stripe alerts for tenant_id org_8f92a after deploying release_version v2.4 (feature_flag FF_NEW_BILLING). The customer (frank@bigcorp.com) just emailed complaining about 3x charges for their monthly renewal ($4,500 total). [14:05] Looks like the billing-db threw exception_type ConnectionTimeout during the transaction. Can someone check the idempotency keys? Logs show request_id=req_12345.

sections.summary: Tenant org_8f92a was charged multiple times for monthly renewal.
sections.impact: Customer overcharged $4,500 total, blowing up support.

title: Mobile app crash on login
raw_text: Since the release_version v2.4 roll out at 9 AM, iOS users are reporting crash on launch. User sarah_99 (sarah.jenkins@email.com) sent a screen recording. Sentry is showing a spike in exception_type EXC_BAD_ACCESS in the AuthViewController. It seems the new JWT token payload is missing the 'roles' array for legacy users. Impact is about 400 users so far, mostly on iOS 16.

sections.summary: iOS app crashing on launch for legacy users due to JWT token.
sections.impact: 400 users unable to log in on iOS 16.

title: Kafka backlog causing webhook delays
raw_text: PagerDuty alert: Kafka lag on topic `webhook-deliveries` exceeded 50,000 messages. Starting around 03:00 UTC. One of our downstream partners, WebhookCorp (IP: 198.51.100.14), is rate limiting us with 429s, causing the workers to stall. We might need to pause consumption for their partition so other tenants aren't affected. John Smith is currently looking at the consumer group metrics.

sections.summary: Kafka topic webhook-deliveries is lagging by 50k messages.
sections.impact: Webhook delivery delays for all tenants due to one partitioned partner.

title: Database connection pool exhaustion leading to SLA risk
raw_text: [08:14] alert-bot: P99 latency for API on Europe-2 region is at 4.5s. [08:15] @dba-team The database connection pool seems exhausted. A few enterprise customers (tenant: acme-corp) reported timeouts on the checkout page. We're scaling up the read replicas now to mitigate, but this might breach our SLA if it continues for another hour. Potential SLA penalty is around 5000 EUR if not resolved quickly. Can someone on the DBA team check the locking queries? I pinged Sarah (sarah.k@system.internal) already.

sections.summary: Database connection pool exhausted causing API timeouts.
sections.impact: Enterprise customers experiencing checkout timeouts. Risk of SLA breach (5000 EUR).

title: Internal wiki search returning blank results
raw_text: [10:30] A few people from the marketing team reported that the internal wiki search (Confluence instance) is returning blank pages. They can still browse by links. I checked the search indexer logs, and there's an elasticsearch OutOfMemory error. It's not blocking any external customers, but we should probably restart the search pod. Impact is low, internal annoyance mostly. We might spend $50 to upscale the node if this persists, but let's try a simple restart first.

sections.summary: Internal wiki search fails with blank pages due to OOM.
sections.impact: Internal marketing team unable to search wiki. No external impact.
